# VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform Migration Plan

## Problem
Migrate 5 MSTest test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).
Azure DevOps CI uses the `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, and code coverage.
All projects use MSTest 3.8.0 on .NET 8 SDK.

## Approach
Centralize MTP configuration in `Directory.Build.props`, install required MTP
extension packages, update the Azure DevOps pipeline from `VSTest@3` to
`DotNetCoreCLI@2`, and translate CLI arguments to MTP syntax.

---

## Pre-Migration Baseline

1. Run `dotnet test` and record total test counts (passed / failed / skipped)
   per project. Save this as the baseline to compare after migration.

---

## Phase 1 — Enable MTP Runner (Directory.Build.props)

Create or update `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root with these
properties. Use a naming-convention condition to scope to test projects only.

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>

    <!-- Enable dotnet test integration on .NET 8 SDK -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Adjust the condition** to match your actual project naming convention
> (e.g., `.EndsWith('Tests')`, `.Contains('.Test')`). If names vary, add
> multiple conditions or set the properties per-project instead.

> **Important**: `IsTestProject` is NOT available in `Directory.Build.props` —
> never use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"`.

### Why these properties?

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `EnableMSTestRunner` | Activates the MTP runner in MSTest 3.2.0+ |
| `OutputType Exe` | Required — MTP test projects must be executables |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Enables `dotnet test` ↔ MTP bridge on .NET 8/9 SDK |

---

## Phase 2 — Install MTP Extension NuGet Packages

You currently use TRX reporting and code coverage. These capabilities are
delivered via extension packages on MTP (they're no longer built into the
runner). Add these to each test `.csproj`, or centralize in
`Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management:

```xml
<!-- TRX report generation (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

Optional — add only if you use crash/hang dump collection today:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.2" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump" Version="1.6.2" />
```

---

## Phase 3 — Translate CLI Arguments

On .NET 8 SDK, MTP arguments must go after a `--` separator.

| Before (VSTest) | After (MTP on .NET 8) |
|------------------|-----------------------|
| `dotnet test --logger trx` | `dotnet test -- --report-trx` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Foo"` | `dotnet test -- --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Foo"` |
| `dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `dotnet test -- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` |
| `dotnet test --blame-crash` | `dotnet test -- --crashdump` |
| `dotnet test --settings test.runsettings` | `dotnet test -- --settings test.runsettings` |
| `dotnet test --results-directory $(dir)` | `dotnet test -- --results-directory $(dir)` |
| `dotnet test -t` | `dotnet test -- --list-tests` |

> **Note**: Build-side arguments (`-c Release`, `--no-build`, `--nologo`, `-v`)
> stay **before** the `--` separator.

Combined example:

```shell
dotnet test -c Release --no-build -- --report-trx --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
```

---

## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

```yaml
# ───── BEFORE ─────
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# ───── AFTER ─────
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --no-build
      --
      --report-trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage
      --coverage-output-format cobertura

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

> **Key change**: `VSTest@3` discovers DLLs and invokes `vstest.console.exe`.
> `DotNetCoreCLI@2` with `command: test` invokes `dotnet test`, which now
> routes to MTP. You must publish TRX and coverage results explicitly with
> separate tasks.

### If you invoke vstest.console.exe in scripts

Replace any direct `vstest.console.exe` calls with `dotnet test`. The test
projects are now executables and can also be run directly:

```shell
./MyProject.Tests/bin/Release/net8.0/MyProject.Tests
```

---

## Phase 5 — Handle Behavioral Differences

### Zero-tests exit code

VSTest silently succeeds when zero tests are discovered. MTP returns
**exit code 8**, which will fail your CI build. If you have conditional test
discovery (e.g., category filters that sometimes match nothing), handle this:

**Option A** — In `Directory.Build.props` (recommended):

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
    $(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8
  </TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Option B** — Pipeline environment variable:

```yaml
env:
  TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE: '8'
```

---

## Phase 6 — Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after verification)

Once migration is verified end-to-end, remove from each test project:

```xml
<!-- REMOVE these -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
```

`MSTest.TestAdapter` is the VSTest adapter — it is not needed on MTP.
`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` provides the VSTest hosting — also not needed.

> **Keep** `MSTest.TestFramework` (or `MSTest` metapackage) — that's the
> assertion/attribute library, still required.

---

## Phase 7 — Verify

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match pre-migration baseline
3. Run one test executable directly:
   `./MyProject.Tests/bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe`
4. CI pipeline produces TRX files and code coverage artifacts
5. Visual Studio / VS Code Test Explorer discovers and runs tests
6. No `vstest.console.exe` invocations remain anywhere

---

## Checklist

- [ ] Pre-migration baseline recorded
- [ ] `Directory.Build.props` created/updated with MTP properties
- [ ] `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` added to all 5 projects
- [ ] `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` added to all 5 projects
- [ ] Azure DevOps pipeline updated (VSTest@3 → DotNetCoreCLI@2)
- [ ] PublishTestResults@2 and PublishCodeCoverageResults@2 tasks added
- [ ] CLI arguments translated (--logger trx → --report-trx, etc.)
- [ ] Zero-tests exit code 8 handled (if applicable)
- [ ] `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` removed
- [ ] Test counts match baseline
- [ ] Direct executable run works
- [ ] CI pipeline green with TRX + coverage artifacts

---

## Common Pitfalls

| Pitfall | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| MTP args ignored on .NET 8 | Must use `--` separator: `dotnet test -- --report-trx` |
| Exit code 8 in CI | MTP fails on zero tests; use `--ignore-exit-code 8` |
| Missing `OutputType Exe` | Required for MTP — set in `Directory.Build.props` |
| `IsTestProject` condition in Directory.Build.props | Not available at import time — use `MSBuildProjectName` condition |
| Mixing VSTest + MTP projects | Unsupported — migrate all 5 projects together |
| Keeping MSTest.TestAdapter | Remove it — it's VSTest-only and can conflict with MTP |

---

## Future Considerations

- When upgrading to **.NET 10 SDK**, replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`
  with a `global.json` `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }`
  section and drop the `--` separator from CLI invocations.
- Consider adopting **MSTest.Sdk** for a cleaner project file (it sets
  `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and package references automatically).

## References

- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [MTP overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)

---

Create or update `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root with these
properties. Use a naming-convention condition to scope to test projects only.

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>

    <!-- Enable dotnet test integration on .NET 8 SDK -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Adjust the condition** to match your actual project naming convention
> (e.g., `.EndsWith('Tests')`, `.Contains('.Test')`). If names vary, add
> multiple conditions or set the properties per-project instead.

> **Important**: `IsTestProject` is NOT available in `Directory.Build.props` —
> never use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"`.

### Why these properties?

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `EnableMSTestRunner` | Activates the MTP runner in MSTest 3.2.0+ |
| `OutputType Exe` | Required — MTP test projects must be executables |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Enables `dotnet test` ↔ MTP bridge on .NET 8/9 SDK |

---

## Phase 2 — Install MTP Extension NuGet Packages

You currently use TRX reporting and code coverage. These capabilities are
delivered via extension packages on MTP (they're no longer built into the
runner). Add these to each test `.csproj`, or centralize in
`Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management:

<!-- TRX report generation (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

Optional — add only if you use crash/hang dump collection today:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump" Version="1.6.2" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump" Version="1.6.2" />
```

---

## Phase 3 — Translate CLI Arguments

On .NET 8 SDK, MTP arguments must go after a `--` separator.

|------------------|-----------------------|
| `dotnet test --logger trx` | `dotnet test -- --report-trx` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Foo"` | `dotnet test -- --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Foo"` |
| `dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `dotnet test -- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` |
| `dotnet test --blame-crash` | `dotnet test -- --crashdump` |
| `dotnet test --settings test.runsettings` | `dotnet test -- --settings test.runsettings` |
| `dotnet test --results-directory $(dir)` | `dotnet test -- --results-directory $(dir)` |
| `dotnet test -t` | `dotnet test -- --list-tests` |

> stay **before** the `--` separator.

      --no-build
      --
      --report-trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)

  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'

  inputs:
